description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

description: Symptom: The switch logs the message, user 'UNKNOWN' from address 'UNKNOWN' through REST session every time when the switch is logged in via REST interface. Scenario: This issue is observed when the switch is managed via AFC/REST and the syslog "auditable-events" is configured with the severity level, INFO.

description: Symptom: An error message "aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator eap-tls-fragment towards-server <max-fragment-size>" was unrecognized was displayed. Scenario: This issue is seen when users configure the following CLI command as a single line into Aruba Central MultiEdit: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator eap-tls-fragment towards-server <max-fragment-size> Workaround: Enter command eap-tls-fragment towards-server in a new line: Copy switch(config-if)# aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator

description: Symptom: Network may experience the effects of MAC address collisions. Scenario: This issue is observed when the system MAC address and active-gateway MAC address are configured to be the same. Workaround: It is not recommended to configure the same MAC address as system MAC address and active-gateway MAC address.
symptom: Network may experience the effects of MAC address collisions.
scenario: This issue is observed when the system MAC address and active-gateway MAC address are configured to be the same.
workaround: It is not recommended to configure the same MAC address as system MAC address and active-gateway MAC address.

description: Symptom: IGMP/MLD control packets were not processed and the querier information was not learnt. Scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.
symptom: IGMP/MLD control packets were not processed and the querier information was not learnt.

workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: An error message "aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator eap-tls-fragment towards-server <max-fragment-size>" was unrecognized was displayed. Scenario: This issue is seen when users configure the following CLI command as a single line into Aruba Central MultiEdit: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator eap-tls-fragment towards-server <max-fragment-size> Workaround: Enter command eap-tls-fragment towards-server in a new line: Copy switch(config-if)# aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator

description: Symptom: Network may experience the effects of MAC address collisions. Scenario: This issue is observed when the system MAC address and active-gateway MAC address are configured to be the same. Workaround: It is not recommended to configure the same MAC address as system MAC address and active-gateway MAC address.

description: Symptom: A delay was observed during the VSX software upgrade. Scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.
symptom: A delay was observed during the VSX software upgrade.
scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration.
workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.

messages.content: What is the symptom of Bug 292867?

VSX (Bug ID 292867): Symptom: Delay during VSX software upgrade. Scenario: Continuous multicast control packets arrive through VLANs without IGMP/MLD configuration in a VXLAN setup. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all VLANs with multicast clients.
